Angus Gunn.bio: English-born Scottish international goalkeeper who chose to represent Scotland over England after initially earning one cap for the Three Lions. Son of former Norwich and Celtic keeper Bryan Gunn, he has been a solid presence at Norwich City in the Championship.
Angus Gunn.playingStyle: Tall, commanding goalkeeper with excellent reflexes and shot-stopping ability who dominates his penalty area and is comfortable with the ball at his feet.
Angus Gunn.worldCup2026Role: First-choice goalkeeper expected to start in Scotland's group stage matches.
Angus Gunn.notableFact: Won one cap for England in 2018 before switching allegiance to Scotland, following in the footsteps of his father Bryan who was a Scottish international.

Anthony Ralston.bio: Celtic academy graduate and reliable right-back who has become a regular in both Celtic's domestic dominance and Scotland's international setup. A tenacious defender who gives everything on the pitch.
Anthony Ralston.playingStyle: Industrious, overlapping right-back who combines solid defensive work with tireless runs forward and delivers dangerous crosses into the box.
Anthony Ralston.worldCup2026Role: Backup right-back option providing energy and attacking width from the bench.
Anthony Ralston.notableFact: Came through Celtic's academy and has won multiple Scottish Premiership titles, becoming a fan favorite for his wholehearted commitment.

Andrew Robertson.bio: Scotland's captain and one of the best left-backs in world football, having been a cornerstone of Liverpool's success under Jurgen Klopp including their Champions League and Premier League triumphs. Rose from lower-league football with Queen's Park and Dundee United to the pinnacle of the game.
Andrew Robertson.playingStyle: Tireless attacking left-back with exceptional crossing ability, relentless overlapping runs, and the stamina to dominate the entire left flank for 90 minutes.
Andrew Robertson.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back and captain, Scotland's most important player and leader who will drive the team forward from the back.
Andrew Robertson.notableFact: Rose from playing amateur football with Queen's Park to winning the Champions League with Liverpool, one of the most remarkable career arcs in modern football.

Scott McTominay.bio: Box-to-box midfielder who made a bold move from Manchester United to Napoli in Serie A, where he has thrived as a goal-scoring midfielder. A physical presence who combines power with an increasingly potent eye for goal.
Scott McTominay.playingStyle: Powerful, dynamic box-to-box midfielder who arrives late in the box to score goals, wins aerial duels, and covers enormous ground with his running.
Scott McTominay.worldCup2026Role: Starting central midfielder and goal threat from deep, Scotland's most potent attacking weapon from midfield.
Scott McTominay.notableFact: Transformed into a prolific goal-scoring midfielder at Napoli, scoring regularly from central midfield after years of more defensive duties at Manchester United.

Grant Hanley.bio: Experienced center-back and former Scotland captain who has spent the bulk of his career in England's Championship with Norwich City. A no-nonsense defender who brings organization and aerial power.
Grant Hanley.playingStyle: Traditional, physical center-back who excels in the air, makes crucial blocks and clearances, and organizes the defensive line with vocal leadership.
Grant Hanley.worldCup2026Role: Experienced backup center-back who provides squad depth and tournament experience from the bench.
Grant Hanley.notableFact: Has captained Scotland on multiple occasions and has been a consistent presence in the squad across multiple qualifying campaigns.

Callum McGregor.bio: Celtic captain and the heartbeat of Scottish domestic football, McGregor is a technically gifted midfielder who has been instrumental in Celtic's sustained dominance of the Scottish Premiership under Ange Postecoglou and beyond.
Callum McGregor.playingStyle: Intelligent, metronomic central midfielder who keeps possession ticking with quick, short passes, rarely loses the ball, and dictates the tempo of matches.
Callum McGregor.worldCup2026Role: Starting midfielder who will control possession and set the rhythm for Scotland alongside McTominay and McGinn.
Callum McGregor.notableFact: Has won over 10 consecutive Scottish Premiership titles with Celtic, making him one of the most decorated players in Scottish football history.

John McGinn.bio: Energetic midfielder who has been a key player for Aston Villa in the Premier League and one of Scotland's most important creative forces. A fan favorite known for his powerful shooting, driving runs, and passionate celebrations.
John McGinn.playingStyle: Dynamic, aggressive midfielder who drives forward with the ball, unleashes powerful shots from distance, and presses opponents with ferocious intensity.
John McGinn.worldCup2026Role: Starting midfielder forming a potent central trio with McTominay and McGregor, providing energy, goals, and creativity.
John McGinn.notableFact: Scored a spectacular overhead kick against Spain and has 16 international goals, making him one of Scotland's most prolific midfielders in the modern era.

Stuart Armstrong.bio: Versatile attacking midfielder who spent productive years in the Premier League with Southampton before returning to Scotland with Aberdeen. A clever player who can unlock defenses with his movement and passing.
Stuart Armstrong.playingStyle: Intelligent, mobile attacking midfielder who finds pockets of space between the lines, links play with neat passing, and contributes goals from advanced positions.
Stuart Armstrong.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player who provides tactical flexibility and can play across midfield and attacking positions off the bench.
Stuart Armstrong.notableFact: Played in the Premier League for several seasons with Southampton before returning to Scottish football with Aberdeen.

Lyndon Dykes.bio: Australian-born Scottish international striker who chose to represent Scotland through his parentage. A powerful target man at QPR in the Championship who gives defenders a physical battle for the full 90 minutes.
Lyndon Dykes.playingStyle: Strong, combative target man who holds the ball up, wins aerial duels, and brings others into play with his back-to-goal work and physical presence.
Lyndon Dykes.worldCup2026Role: Squad striker who provides an alternative physical option up front, useful as a plan B to change the attacking dynamic.
Lyndon Dykes.notableFact: Born and raised in Australia's Gold Coast before choosing to play for Scotland, qualifying through his father who was born in Edinburgh.

Che Adams.bio: Quick, versatile forward who made a successful move from Southampton to Torino in Serie A, where he has impressed with his work rate, pressing, and clinical finishing. Born in Leicester and qualified for Scotland through his grandfather.
Che Adams.playingStyle: Mobile, hard-working striker who presses from the front, drops deep to link play, and possesses sharp finishing ability inside the box.
Che Adams.worldCup2026Role: Starting striker and Scotland's primary goal threat, expected to lead the line with his movement and pressing energy.
Che Adams.notableFact: Qualified for Scotland through his grandfather and has become one of the team's most important players after a successful transition to Serie A football.

Ryan Christie.bio: Versatile attacking midfielder who has established himself as a regular in the Premier League with Bournemouth after earlier spells at Celtic and Aberdeen. An energetic player who contributes at both ends of the pitch.
Ryan Christie.playingStyle: High-energy attacking midfielder who presses aggressively, makes intelligent runs beyond the striker, and possesses a dangerous shot from outside the box.
Ryan Christie.worldCup2026Role: Versatile attacking option who can play across the front line or in midfield, offering energy and directness off the bench or in rotation.
Ryan Christie.notableFact: Has played over 45 times for Scotland and scored crucial qualifying goals across multiple campaigns.

Craig Gordon.bio: Legendary Scottish goalkeeper who has defied age to remain in the international setup well into his 40s. A former British record transfer when he joined Sunderland in 2007, he has experienced incredible career highs and lows including serious injuries.
Craig Gordon.playingStyle: Experienced, agile goalkeeper with exceptional shot-stopping ability and the reflexes of a much younger player, capable of producing stunning saves in big moments.
Craig Gordon.worldCup2026Role: Veteran third-choice goalkeeper providing invaluable dressing room experience and mentorship to the younger keepers.
Craig Gordon.notableFact: At 43 years old, he would be one of the oldest players in World Cup history if he features, having first been capped for Scotland back in 2004.

Jack Hendry.bio: Well-traveled center-back who has played across Europe including spells in Belgium, Italy, and now the Saudi Pro League with Al-Ettifaq. A physical defender who has grown in stature within the national team setup.
Jack Hendry.playingStyle: Tall, athletic center-back who is strong in the air, comfortable stepping forward with the ball, and aggressive in one-on-one defending situations.
Jack Hendry.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back who brings international experience from playing across multiple European leagues.
Jack Hendry.notableFact: Has played club football in six different countries across his career, giving him a broad range of tactical experience.

Billy Gilmour.bio: Technically gifted young midfielder who emerged through Chelsea's academy before making a high-profile move to Napoli alongside McTominay. A diminutive playmaker with vision and composure beyond his years.
Billy Gilmour.playingStyle: Elegant, technically refined midfielder who controls tempo with precise passing, glides past pressure with quick turns, and reads the game intelligently despite his slight build.
Billy Gilmour.worldCup2026Role: Key midfield option who can start or come off the bench to control possession and change the tempo of matches.
Billy Gilmour.notableFact: Was named Man of the Match on his full international debut for Scotland and plays alongside compatriot Scott McTominay at Napoli in Serie A.

Liam Cooper.bio: Experienced center-back who captained Leeds United through their memorable promotion to the Premier League under Marcelo Bielsa. A dependable, no-frills defender who leads by example.
Liam Cooper.playingStyle: Committed, vocal center-back who excels at marshaling the defensive line, making last-ditch blocks, and winning physical battles with opposing strikers.
Liam Cooper.worldCup2026Role: Experienced defensive squad member providing cover at center-back and leadership within the group.
Liam Cooper.notableFact: Captained Leeds United to the Premier League under Marcelo Bielsa and was born in Aberdeen before qualifying for Scotland.

Lewis Ferguson.bio: Dynamic midfielder who made a successful move from Aberdeen to Bologna in Serie A, where he has thrived and earned recognition as one of the best Scottish exports to Italian football. A box-to-box player with an impressive goal record.
Lewis Ferguson.playingStyle: Energetic box-to-box midfielder who contributes goals and assists, times his runs into the penalty area well, and provides relentless running and pressing.
Lewis Ferguson.worldCup2026Role: Important midfield rotation option who brings Serie A quality and a goal threat to Scotland's central midfield group.
Lewis Ferguson.notableFact: Quickly established himself as a regular starter at Bologna in Serie A, scoring important goals in European competition.

Stuart Findlay.bio: Reliable center-back who has been a steady performer for Kilmarnock in the Scottish Premiership. A tough, uncompromising defender who has earned his place in the squad through consistent domestic performances.
Stuart Findlay.playingStyle: Physical, rugged center-back who prioritizes defensive solidity, wins aerial duels, and makes timely tackles and interceptions.
Stuart Findlay.worldCup2026Role: Depth option at center-back who provides cover and competition for places within the defensive group.
Stuart Findlay.notableFact: Spent time in the United States with Portland Timbers before returning to Scotland and establishing himself at Kilmarnock.

Aaron Hickey.bio: Highly talented young defender who made his name at Hearts before moving to Bologna in Serie A as a teenager, eventually earning a move to Brentford in the Premier League. Uniquely comfortable with both feet, making him tactically versatile.
Aaron Hickey.playingStyle: Modern, attack-minded full-back who is genuinely two-footed, allowing him to play on either flank and cut inside or overlap with equal effectiveness.
Aaron Hickey.worldCup2026Role: Versatile defensive option who can cover both full-back positions, providing valuable flexibility to the squad.
Aaron Hickey.notableFact: Is genuinely ambidextrous with both feet, a rare trait that has allowed him to play at both left-back and right-back at the highest level.

Lawrence Shankland.bio: Prolific Scottish striker who has been a consistent goal-scorer in the Scottish Premiership with Hearts after earlier spells at Dundee United. A natural finisher with an instinct for being in the right place at the right time.
Lawrence Shankland.playingStyle: Clinical penalty-box striker with sharp movement, excellent positioning, and the composure to finish chances with either foot or his head.
Lawrence Shankland.worldCup2026Role: Impact striker off the bench who can change games with his predatory instincts inside the penalty area.
Lawrence Shankland.notableFact: Has been one of the top scorers in the Scottish Premiership across multiple seasons and scored on his Scotland debut.

Ross McCrorie.bio: Versatile defender who can operate at right-back or in central midfield, developed at Rangers before moving to Bristol City in the Championship. A diligent, hard-working player who offers tactical flexibility.
Ross McCrorie.playingStyle: Disciplined, versatile defender who can play across the back line or in midfield, combining solid defensive positioning with clean, efficient passing.
Ross McCrorie.worldCup2026Role: Utility squad player who provides cover across multiple defensive and midfield positions.
Ross McCrorie.notableFact: Twin brother of Robby McCrorie, who is also a professional footballer playing as a goalkeeper in Scotland.

Kenny McLean.bio: Dependable central midfielder who has been a consistent performer at Norwich City in the Championship. A steady, experienced presence who provides balance and discipline in midfield.
Kenny McLean.playingStyle: Calm, composed central midfielder who keeps the ball moving efficiently, provides defensive cover, and rarely makes costly mistakes in possession.
Kenny McLean.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield squad player who can fill in as a holding or box-to-box midfielder when needed.
Kenny McLean.notableFact: Has been a consistent presence in Scotland squads across multiple qualifying campaigns and scored a memorable goal against Israel.

Liam Kelly.bio: Rangers goalkeeper who has worked his way into the Scotland setup through consistent performances in the Scottish Premiership. A solid, dependable keeper who competes for backup minutes behind the first choice.
Liam Kelly.playingStyle: Traditional goalkeeper with good shot-stopping, reliable handling, and the ability to organize his defense through vocal communication.
Liam Kelly.worldCup2026Role: Second or third-choice goalkeeper providing competition and depth in the goalkeeping department.
Liam Kelly.notableFact: Has represented Scotland at various youth levels before earning his place in the senior squad through his form at Rangers.

Kieran Tierney.bio: Highly talented left-back who became one of Celtic's youngest-ever debutants before moving to Arsenal in the Premier League. Despite injury setbacks, his quality on the ball and attacking drive make him one of Scotland's most gifted defenders, now back at Celtic.
Kieran Tierney.playingStyle: Technically skilled, attack-minded left-back who excels at overlapping runs, delivers pinpoint crosses, and defends with aggressive intensity and bravery.
Kieran Tierney.worldCup2026Role: Key left-back option competing with Robertson for the starting spot, capable of playing as a left center-back in a back three.
Kieran Tierney.notableFact: Became Celtic's youngest-ever debutant at 17 and won four Scottish Premiership titles before his 22nd birthday.

Greg Taylor.bio: Celtic's consistent left-back who has been a dependable performer in both domestic and European competition. A steady, reliable defender who complements the more attacking options at full-back in Scotland's squad.
Greg Taylor.playingStyle: Disciplined, defensively solid left-back who maintains good positioning, makes overlapping runs when the opportunity arises, and delivers consistent performances.
Greg Taylor.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice left-back providing depth behind Robertson and Tierney, available for rotation in group matches.
Greg Taylor.notableFact: Has been part of Celtic's dominant run in Scottish football and featured in Champions League group stage matches.

Ryan Porteous.bio: Athletic center-back who emerged at Hibernian before moving to Watford in the Championship. A physically imposing defender who plays on the front foot and is not afraid to step forward aggressively.
Ryan Porteous.playingStyle: Aggressive, front-foot center-back who steps up to win the ball, carries it forward when possible, and uses his physicality to dominate opposing strikers.
Ryan Porteous.worldCup2026Role: Young center-back option who can provide energy and aggression in defense when called upon.
Ryan Porteous.notableFact: Attracted interest from several clubs across Europe due to his aggressive, modern style of center-back play before settling at Watford.

James Forrest.bio: Celtic legend and one of the longest-serving players in Scottish football, Forrest has spent his entire career at Celtic Park winning numerous league titles. A rapid winger whose pace and directness have been a weapon for over a decade.
James Forrest.playingStyle: Pacey, direct winger who hugs the touchline, uses his speed to beat defenders on the outside, and delivers dangerous crosses and cut-backs from wide positions.
James Forrest.worldCup2026Role: Experienced wide option who can provide pace and directness off the bench in the later stages of matches.
James Forrest.notableFact: Has won over 10 consecutive league titles with Celtic and scored the crucial goal against Israel that sent Scotland to Euro 2024 qualifying playoffs.
